addTrack.title: Add New Track
addTrack.shortNameHint: Auto-filled from the track name — edit it if you'd like.

sectors.guidance: Add a sector to start splitting the lap. Mark exactly three lines (start/finish + two) as Major sectors to save.

visual.drawingHelps: Drawing an outline helps on-device course detection.
visual.drawStart: Click on the map to start drawing the track layout
visual.drawPoints_one: {{count}} point — click to add more, Undo to remove last
visual.drawPoints_other: {{count}} points — click to add more, Undo to remove last
visual.dragMarkers: Drag the markers to adjust the {{name}} line — changes save automatically when you release
visual.geoUnavailableDesc: Your browser does not support geolocation
visual.notEnoughGpsDesc: Insufficient GPS points for a drawing
visual.tooShort: The GPS trace was too short or stationary.

prompt.addCoursePrompt: Add a course to {{track}} to enable lap timing.
prompt.waypointNotice: No known track matched this data. Lap times are estimated using a GPS waypoint — accuracy is lower than normal.
prompt.noTrackNotice: No known track matches this GPS data. Create a new track, then add a course to enable lap timing.

submit.confirmTitle: Share your tracks with the community
submit.confirmDesc: Contribute the tracks and courses you have created. The track collection lives in the RacePlex repo — no account needed.
submit.li1: We work out which of your tracks and courses are not in the collection yet, and build the file for each one.
submit.li2: You get the file contents. Open a track submission issue with one click, or open the pull request yourself.
submit.li3: A maintainer reviews it, and automated checks confirm the timing lines actually work.
submit.li4: Once merged, your track ships with the app for everyone. Nothing is uploaded to a server.
submit.reviewDesc: These are the tracks and courses that will go into your contribution.
submit.nothingNew: Everything you have created is already in the collection. Nothing new to contribute.
submit.contributeDesc: One file per track. Open an issue and we will handle the rest, or add the file yourself and open a pull request.
submit.prNote: Prefer git? Drop the file in <c>tracks/</c>, run <c>bun run build:tracks</c>, and open a pull request.
submit.toastCopyFailed: Could not copy to the clipboard
submit.toastPasteNeeded: JSON copied — paste it into the form
submit.toastPasteNeededDesc: Your drawn outline is too long to prefill, so paste it into the Track JSON box.

trackEditor.submitTooltipEmpty: Nothing new to share right now — create or edit a track/course (or add a drawing) and it'll show up here to contribute.
trackEditor.submitTooltip: Sharing your track configurations to the database helps the project grow — your tracks become available to everyone, so the community spends less time mapping and more time driving.
trackEditor.submitGift: Signed-in contributions earn free cloud storage
